name: conductor-epsilon-greedy-pattern
description: Conductor wraps Brain with epsilon-greedy prompt selection (EPSILON_START=0.3 decaying to EPSILON_MIN=0.05 over DECAY_PER=50 outcomes). build_prompt() returns (text, prompt_id, context_tokens) and writes metadata to .prism/brain/.conductor_meta.json. Access brain._scores directly for raw queries. Difficulty from avg_tokens + AC count regex.

name: conductor-record-outcome-load-prompt-id
description: record_outcome() uses _load_prompt_id() OR self.last_prompt_id to get persisted prompt_id — _load_prompt_id() handles cross-process boundaries correctly.

name: conductor-epsilon-greedy-implementation
description: Conductor._select_prompt() implements epsilon-greedy: calls brain.outcome_count() for total_runs, computes epsilon via exp decay (0.3→0.05), random variant with prob epsilon, brain.best_prompt() otherwise. record_outcome uses _load_prompt_id() (disk-persisted) with fallback to in-memory last_prompt_id. Prompt variant text injected into prism_loop_context after role card.

name: conductor-canopy-sync
description: _sync_canopy_variants() runs at Conductor init after Brain.__init__(); reads .canopy/prompts.jsonl via git rev-parse --show-toplevel anchor; upserts into brain._scores prompt_variants table with source='canopy'.

name: conductor-variant-retirement
description: _check_retirement() called in record_outcome() after brain.record_outcome(); thresholds: avg_score < RETIRE_AVG_SCORE_THRESHOLD (0.3) and total_runs >= RETIRE_MIN_RUNS (5); writes to retired_variants; _random_variant() and _select_prompt() filter retired variants.
